content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


January 26, 1989


Robert Lagomarsino
Member of Congress
814 State Street, Suite 121
Santa Barbara, CA  93101

Re:  FPPC Advice No. A-89-046

Dear Congressman Lagomarsino:

     This is in response to your request on behalf of Michael J. Long for information regarding the campaign reporting obligations of a candidate for elective office of the California Republican Party.  The enclosed letter to Elvin W. Moon answers Mr. Long's questions.

     If Mr. Long has any additional questions, he may contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Kim Marie Taylor
Staff Services Analyst

Enclosure
A-88-461


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

ROBERT J. LAGOMARSINO
19TH DISTRICT, CALIFORNIA

2332 RAYBURN BUILDING
WASHINGTON, DC 20515
202-225-3601

CONFERENCE SECRETARY

CONGRESSIONAL OBSERVER
GENEVA ARMS CONTROL TALKS

CONGRESSIONAL TASK FORCE ON
AFGHANISTAN—CO-CHAIRMAN

POW/MIA TASK FORCE
CHAIRMAN

COMMITTEE ON
FOREIGN AFFAIRS
SUBCOMMITTEES

WESTERN HEMISPHERE AFFAIRS
VICE CHAIRMAN

ASIAN AND PACIFIC AFFAIRS

Congress of the United States
House of Representatives
Washington, DC 20515

COMMITTEE ON
INTERIOR AND INSULAR
AFFAIRS
SUBCOMMITTEES

INSULAR AND INTERNATIONAL AFFAIRS
VICE CHAIRMAN

NATIONAL PARKS AND PUBLIC LANDS
VICE CHAIRMAN—PARKS

December 29, 1988

Gregory Baugher
Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

Dear Mr. Baugher:

    Please find enclosed a letter which I recieved from my
constituent, Michael Long of Santa Barbara, Ca. who is requesting
my assistance with obtaining information from the Fair Political
Practices Commission.

    Your review of this matter and help in providing me with
information that would assist me in reponding to my constituent
would be appreciated.  Please send your response to my Santa
Barbara district office at: 814 State Street, Suite 121, Santa
Barbara, Ca. 93101.

    Thank you for your assistance.

                                    Sincerely,

                                    [signature]
                                    ROBERT J. LAGOMARSINO
                                    Member of Congress


RJL/mjl

Suite 101                          Studio 121 EL PASO              104 E BOONE ST #E
5740 RALSTON                        814 STATE STREET               SANTA MARIA, 93454
VENTURA, 93003                      SANTA BARBARA, 93101           922-2131
805-650-4344                        963-1708

December 27, 1988


Dear Congressman Lagomarsino:

     I am writing to request your assistance in obtaining
the requirements for filing and reporting with the Calif-
ornia Fair Political Pratices Commission for elective
office within the California Republican Party.

     As you may know, California specifically provides
for political parties in the state Constitution, unlike
the U.S. Constitution.  I will be running for an elected
position within the California Republican Party in early
1989 and am unsure as to the filing and reporting require-
ments with the FPPC, particularly since the passage of
Proposition's 68 and 73.

     Specifically, I would like to know, am I as a candidate
for an elected position within the California Republican
Party required to file and or report any funds that I raise
to pay for the campaign; are the individuals, businesses,
PAC's, or corporations required to report their contribu-
tions to my campaign to the FPPC; am I limited to the amounts
that I can raise from the aforementioned; are they limited
to the amounts they can donate, and am I limited to an
overall amount that I can spend?

     As I understand the law, only those running for public,
local, legislative, and state-wide office are required to
report and file with the FPPC.  Your assistance in obtaining
the answers to these questions would be appreciated.  I wish
to begin my campaign immediately and need to advise my
treasurer, and donors.

     Thank you for your assistance Congressman.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Michael J. Long
                                        1615 Olive Street #B
                                        Santa Barbara, Ca. 93101
